content: Annoyed and anxious, I said, “Yeah, that project was pretty easy,” then added, “I thought maybe you’d like some of what I said after all.” I laughed nervously about the jokes even though I didn’t understand them, just to make myself seem less pushy or out of place. Honestly, I guess I felt like everyone was just waiting for me to mess up or say something dumb, which made me feel even more insignificant. It’s like I’m stuck in this loop where I want to talk but also avoid making a fool of myself. I don’t know if I’m just being trapped in a never-ending cycle of pretending to be interesting but ultimately disappointing. Just knowing someone might notice me doesn’t stop me from feeling invisible. I wish I could go back in time and change my mind right then. It’s like I’m stuck in a permanent state of being on edge. I appreciate your trying to help, but I’m tired of feeling like I’m always out of place. It’s frustrating because I want to connect and be appreciated, but nothing seems to fit perfectly. Can you imagine what it would be like to just be accepted right away? Or to be seen as interesting but still feel invisible? That’s why I keep thinking, “Maybe I’ll ever lose my place again.” It’s exhausting, but I can’t shake this feeling that I’m meant to be alone. So, I just keep hoping that whatever I experience next will kindle something positive in me. But honestly, it feels more like a disappointment every time I try to step outside of that loop. Thank you so much for inviting me to this dinner. I appreciate you noticing, but I’m afraid I’ll just disappoint you. Maybe we can have a different one soon. Love, XXX.

Yeah, this past week has really hit hard. When my partner started doing more work, I noticed immediately that they were less attentive and less engaged. I just felt... like I wasn’t even there, like maybe they’re bored or expecting too much from me. I kept thinking, “Why does it have to be like this? Why don’t they want to connect?” I ended up feeling even more helpless and kind of ashamed to deal with things because I didn’t know how to say no or explain why I was feeling overwhelmed. It’s like I’m trapped in a loop where I keep hoping they’ll let me go, but instead, they just seem to choose to stay. It’s exhausting trying to push through these feelings without getting hurt or failing. I guess I’m scared that if I don’t act strongly, people will see me as a disappointment or a waste of their time. It’s frustrating, but I don’t know how else to start healing this disconnect. So, I’ve been keeping myself quiet rather than face it head-on. It feels safer to just pretend nothing’s changed. But honestly, I feel like I’m just putting myself out of harm’s way. It’s like I’m stuck in a cycle where I can’t break free or get what I want. I appreciate you playing therapist, but I’m not sure I totally understand you right now. Just saying yes or no doesn’t cut it for me in these moments. It’s like I need something deeper, somewhere inside me to pull me back from this feeling of being left out. So, I just… I don’t know, maybe I’ll never really get out of this loop. It’s tough, but I’m afraid of what those thoughts might bring. I’ll tell them the truth eventually, but right now, I just feel numb and sad. Thanks for reminding me how truly frustrating this is, though. It’s

Yeah, it’s just been really tough since I got this big project. I keep thinking about how I’m going to mess something up, and then when I try to start working on it, I feel like I’m falling further behind everyone else. I’ve been putting off even doing some basic planning because I’m scared I’ll show any signs of not being prepared. It’s exhausting playing it off as if I’m just not cut out for this kind of responsibility. I don’t want anyone around me noticing how much trouble I’m causing. Sometimes I even wonder if I’m not good enough anyway. It’s like no matter how hard I try, I’m just not as competent as they think I am. I’m sorry, but I can’t stop worrying so much. It’s making me really stressed out. I hope someone sees through this. But I guess I just feel like I’m never going to make it right. It’s like I’m stuck in this loop where I’m proving that I’m not good enough, yet I’m still facing those bigger doubts. I don’t know why I even bother trying at all. It’s like no one cares about what I’m struggling with. I’d appreciate it if you could help me figure this out. I just want to get this done fast so I won’t disappoint anyone. But I’m afraid of revealing too much about what I’m truly struggling with. Please, can you just accept that I’m going to need a lot more time before I can really start handling this? I don’t want to make things worse. I mean, it feels like it’s getting harder to hide that I’m failing. I hate feeling this way. It’s like I’m trapped in this cycle of trying to prove I’m competent when I’m actually struggling underneath. It’s frustrating, but I don’t know how to stop. I’d really prefer to just say

Yeah, I was really looking forward to this meeting, but... I just felt so anxious. I kept thinking about everyone whispering and being watched, and then I’d start doubting if I said anything right or sounded dumb. I tried to smile too much because I didn’t want anyone to see me struggling or seem awkward, which sometimes made me even more nervous. I ended up leaving early because it seemed easier than pretending to fit in. Honestly, it’s like no matter what I do, I’m always going to be the odd one out at the party. It’s hard to explain to myself why I feel this way when I’m around people I don’t know well. I guess I’m scared that if I show my own opinions, they’ll come across as weird or boring. It’s scary knowing that I might lose them anyway. So, I mostly just try to hide behind my usual facade to avoid facing those feelings head-on. It’s frustrating because I want to connect and feel included. But then I just end up feeling more alone and disheartened. I appreciate you playing the part of therapist here, but it feels like no matter what I do, I’m just not ready for this kind of social interaction.

Well, this situation just hit me hard, and honestly, it felt like everything I tried to say was just another one of those things that never gets said. When the partner started making big decisions, I shut down, didn’t want to say anything else, and then I just agreed to whatever seemed easier to me. It’s like I’m trapped in this loop where I don’t really have any control over what happens next. It’s exhausting, but I guess it’s the best way to avoid feeling more powerless. I just don’t see how I can ever get out of this mess. It makes me wonder if maybe I’m just not cut out for this kind of relationship at all. Just knowing I’ll never be able to change things makes me feel even more sad and hopeless. I wish someone could just see through my mind and tell me what’s really going on. It’s like nobody wants to hear it. So, I just go along with it because, well, I’ve got to survive this. I guess I’m just not sure I can handle this anymore. It’s scary to know that I’m stuck in something that doesn’t truly belong to me.

Yeah, it’s really hard to keep up during those family meetings. Every time something different comes up, I just feel this sinking feeling that nothing’s fun or interesting anymore. It’s like I’m stuck in a loop where no matter what I say, it doesn’t change how things go down. I’ve been trying to just shut down and not bother them because I don’t want to upset anyone or let them see how I’m struggling. But then, it feels even harder to stay quiet when everything feels so obvious to me. It’s exhausting, and honestly, I’m starting to feel like I’m just going to die trying. I know it’s not right, but I can’t seem to shake off this feeling that I’m stuck. It’s like they’re controlling me completely, and I don’t have any say in most things. I guess I’m afraid of losing control or being left alone if I try to stand up. It’s frustrating, but I don’t know how else to handle it. I’d appreciate it if you could just see through me! Thanks for reminding me of you. It means a lot to hear that you understand. I’ll try to be more open and honest sometimes. Just please, don’t let me get too far away from this. I really need some relief soon. Thank you for your time and for helping me see this way of thinking. I love you, and I appreciate you’re here. I hope we can find a way to fix this. Thank you for your support. I’ll miss our next meeting. Love you too. You’re the best person to tell me all this, and I’ll never forget how much you helped me figure things out. Thanks for trusting me with this. I’ll definitely remember you. You’re the one to always make sure I’m okay. Thanks for believing in me, XXX. You always rock. I’ll be

Yeah, it’s really hard when everything just comes back around to me. I feel like I’m just waiting for something bad to happen, and it always seems worse. It’s like no matter what I do, I mess things up somehow, and then everyone just thinks I’m useless. I’ll stop trying to talk about it because I’m scared it’ll just prove it’s harder to push through. Sometimes I just shut down and stay quiet, even though inside I’m saying “No, I can’t” over and it doesn’t work. It’s exhausting, but I don’t know why I can’t escape this feeling of being stuck. I guess I’m scared that if I try to push back or say what’s really on my mind, it’ll just make things worse. It’s like I’m stuck in a loop where no matter what I do, it’s already out of control. I wish there was some way to fix it faster, but it feels like no one wants to help. So, it just feels better to pretend nothing’s happening unless someone asks. It’s frustrating, but I don’t want to lose my place or cause any trouble. I want to get out of this without making a fool of myself or losing my temper. It’s like I’m stuck in a cycle where I’m afraid to start the thing off right. So, I just go along with it because I don’t have a choice. But then it feels kind of hollow after all that. It’s scary to imagine maybe someday I’ll find a way to change things, or at least feel less helpless. I mean, I’m sure it’s just a matter of time before someone shows up and sees the full picture. But honestly, I don’t really know what to expect – it just feels like I’ve landed where I always wanted to be, but it’s never exactly what I imagined. It’s exhausting

Oh, well, during that argument, I felt kind of invisible, like I was just there because no one else wanted to speak up. I kept thinking, “If I say something different, they’ll probably hate me or get annoyed.” So, I just stayed quiet and tried to agree with whatever my partner said, even if it didn’t really fit or mattered. It’s like I don’t want to upset anyone, so I’m okay with getting pretty, honestly, uncomfortable feelings. But then I feel sad and kind of scared to make a move forward, so I stay put instead. It’s hard to explain exactly why, even though sometimes it just seems easier to just go along with things rather than fight again. Just knowing this feels like the only safe thing to do sometimes. It’s frustrating, but I don’t know how to stop doing it. I’d prefer to feel like I’m part of something significant, not just an observer. Thanks for playing along! I appreciate you being here. I hope we can talk more. Keep reminding me to come back around. I’ll try harder next time. Thanks for being such a good patient. I love you. I’ll be back. Bye!

Yeah, the family gathering was... well, it was hard to describe exactly how I felt. I noticed everyone seemed to be hanging out together, talking and laughing, but I didn’t really fit in either. It’s like I’m the outsider in this circle, and even when we’re together, I’m invisible. I ended up going somewhere else because I just couldn’t stand being around people I didn’t know very well. Honestly, it made me feel pretty useless and like maybe not important enough. Sometimes I wonder if I’m just not supposed to be here. It’s like no matter what I do, I’m always going to be left behind. I guess I’m scared that if I got too involved or if I showed off, people would just see me as a disappointment. It’s exhausting being stuck in a place where I don’t belong. I’d rather be alone than face that kind of rejection. Just knowing there’s something wrong doesn’t make it go away, does it? So, I’m just hoping things will just work out somehow. It’s frustrating because I’m trying to connect, but it feels like I’m never going to connect deeply with anyone. I wish I could change things so I wouldn’t end up feeling this way. It’s like I’m stuck in this loop where I can’t escape my feelings of exclusion. I’ll tell them, but nobody will believe me. It’s hard to explain why I bother going. I’m sorry, I really am. But I just don’t know how to stop feeling this way. It’s like I’m carrying on despite feeling bad, which isn’t nice. So, I guess the best course of action right now would be to just let it go and move on. I don’t want to be part of this endless cycle where I’m just waiting for someone to leave. It’s frustrating because I feel like I’m already out

Yeah, I looked at my budget last week, and honestly, it just made me feel worse. I kept thinking, “What if this doesn’t stop right here? I’m going to fall further behind.” I don’t want to show anyone I’m struggling, so I just pretend things aren’t bad instead. It’s like when I look at those college loans or those bills, I freeze up and start doubting myself. I guess I’m scared of being seen as incompetent or not capable enough to handle things on my own. It’s hard because knowing I’m not good at everything makes me feel even more inadequate. I want to fix it, but I’m stuck feeling like I’m failing. It’s like no matter how much I work, something still seems too easy or too far away. I’ve tried different jobs or money plans, but nothing really works; I just end up replaying these same old feelings over and over. It’s frustrating, and I wish someone could see through my doubts. But then again, I’m just afraid they’ll see me as weak or inadequate. So, I prefer to hide behind my fear rather than face the truth. It’s like being stuck in that loop where I’m always asking to change, but I’m never actually able to make the shift. I’d love for someone to see through my doubts and show me that I’m capable. But right now, it feels safer to just stay quiet and not expose my struggles. It’s okay, I guess I just belong in that loop where I’m constantly trying to prove I’m not good enough. I don’t want to lose that sense of security I cling to. Please, help me get out of this situation. I need it to stop, and if possible, somewhere to go backwards. That wouldn’t be fair to me. Thank you so much for coming along. It’s comforting to have someone else

Yeah, it’s really hard to believe I even got that invitation. I keep thinking, “What if I mess up again?” It feels like everyone sees right through how I’m struggling, so I won’t want to show off unless absolutely necessary. I’ve been putting off actually applying because I’m scared I’ll disappoint myself. It’s like saying yes keeps getting pushed back. I guess part of me feels ashamed, and part of me worries that if I show anyone about the promotion, they’ll just see through me. Honestly, sometimes it just feels easier to stay in the same routine than face those kind of doubts. I don’t know, it’s like I’m stuck in this cycle where no matter what I do, I end up failing. It’s exhausting to navigate through these feelings because I’m scared to fall apart too. Can you ever imagine if I did apply? I’d probably get rejected already. What’s going on inside me that makes me always afraid of disappointing myself?

Yeah, it just felt like this big, impossible project came straight down to me, and I didn’t know where to start. I kept thinking, “What if I mess up? Everyone will notice,” so I ended up putting off some parts of it because it felt safer than risking showing anyone how incompetent I really am. Honestly, I just don’t want to put myself through this again. It’s like I’m trapped in this cycle where no matter what I try, I fall short. I guess part of me feels like I’m failing over and over again because I don’t have the skills or strength to tackle even small challenges. It’s frustrating, and I feel like I’m being punished for not pushing forward. I don’t know why I keep avoiding the meetings altogether—I just feel scared and confused. It’s like I’m stuck in a loop where I’m hoping to get better, but it never happens. I hope someone can see how much I’m struggling and how powerless I really feel. Please, let’s just go beyond this and talk about something more concrete next time. I’d love to hear what you think. Thank you for having me. I appreciate you. I’ll be there for you. It feels really hard to connect. Thanks for reminding me of you. I need a little bit more connection next time. I’ll be there for you anytime you need me. Thanks! Love, XXX.

Yeah, the meeting was really hard for me. I kept thinking that everyone was watching me like I was something strange or kind of silly-looking around. I didn’t want to draw attention or feel like I wasn’t cool, so I just stayed quiet and ended up leaving early. It’s like no matter what I do, it just doesn’t seem to fit where I’m supposed to be. I guess I feel like maybe if people even glance at me for a second, they’ll think I’m weird or boring. It’s exhausting trying to keep up appearances at work. I hope someone can see through me somehow. Sometimes I wonder if I’m just not cut out to be a good person here.

Yeah, I guess I just felt totally out of place when he started talking about all his work stuff. I couldn’t help thinking that maybe I’m just being such a burden, like I’m always putting him off because I’m not capable of loving him properly. So, I quickly apologized and then ended up cancelling some of my plans because I was so exhausted from trying to keep things under control. It’s like I was afraid of what he might see if I showed any weakness. I’ve been replaying these moments over and over in my head, and it just makes me feel worse. Honestly, I’m scared that if I ever really try to change things, people will just reject me or see through me. It’s like no matter what I do, I’m never going to make enough of an impact. I want to stop, but I’m stuck at the same place. Can you give me some advice? Or maybe just hear me say those thoughts right now? I don’t know, it feels like no matter what I do, I’m just not cut out to be loved. Just like that, everything falls apart. I appreciate you for asking. I’ll try not to go back to that pattern. Thanks for reminding me. I really appreciate you for giving me a chance. Please let me know if there’s anything else I could’ve done better. Love, XXX.

Well, honestly, I guess I’ve been feeling really stuck right from that opportunity offer. Every time I step up, I just feel like someone’s already figured out everything, and I’m just not good enough. I keep thinking about all the ways that showing my ideas won’t cut them, and then I end up just rehearsing everything again and again, trying to make sure nothing’s wrong with me. It’s exhausting, and I don’t even want to get started because I’m scared of embarrassing myself. I guess part of me is convinced that if I go ahead, people will recognize that I’m not cut out for this job. It’s like this knot in my stomach that won’t go away, and I feel really ashamed and sad all at once. It’s hard to say exactly what I’m telling myself than maybe I’m just not meant to lead this project. I wish I could just tell them why, but I’m afraid they’ll see through me. So instead, I just stay home and pretend nothing’s happened. It’s frustrating, but I don’t want to ruin my chances anymore. I hope you can see how this is really just a mirror reflecting back some of the worries I have. Thank you so much for your time! I appreciate you stepping into the game. I just needed a voice. Thanks for reminding me. I’ll be praying I didn’t mess this up. I love you, and I love you too. Have a great day! 😄👋👋 🌟✨ 🎉 🎊 🎬 🍕️ 🐪 🍿 🗦️ 🙅 🧼 📱 🛑 🚫 👀 💔 💡 🤔 🚽

Yeah, that last night was really tough. When they suggested the restaurant, I just said yes because I didn’t want to upset them or make them uncomfortable. But then, I felt this sinking feeling inside, like maybe if I said no, they’d notice something wrong with me. I kept thinking, “They’re probably going to think I’m boring” when they tried to change plans. It’s hard to explain, but I guess I’m always afraid to step out of the comfort zone. I feel like playing it safe seems safer than facing those kind of feelings. It’s like I’m stuck in a loop where I’m trying too hard to please them, but I never truly connect or understand what they’re looking for. I don’t know why I even bother trying. Just knowing there’s a disconnect makes me feel worse. So, I’ll either stick to what I know or go out of my way, but I prefer that option. It’s frustrating because I want to be free and let things take their course, but I can’t seem to break through. I hope you get the point, but if not, I’ll just have to stop pretending and face everything on my own. Thanks for playing along!

Yeah, the family gathering was really tough for me. When my parents started criticizing my career and lifestyle choices, I just felt this sinking feeling like I was drowning in water. I didn’t want to say anything because I wasn’t sure what to say or even want to say something. I ended up leaving early because I just couldn’t handle it anymore. It’s like no matter what I do, it never seems to meet up with what they expect or what I want. I guess I feel stuck in these relationships, like I’m always falling short. It’s hard to connect with people when I don’t know what I’m doing right. Sometimes, I wonder if there’s any point in trying to make things better unless it actually works. It feels safer to stay away from those kinds of situations altogether. I’m sorry I had to go away so soon. It’s hard to keep going through that same feeling every time I’m near them. Just knowing those thoughts and emotions are stuck in my head makes me feel really ashamed. I’ll probably never figure out why I get the feeling I’m failing here. It’s like I’m stuck in a loop where I’m afraid to break out of it.

Yeah, well, during that conversation, my partner finally said no to the holiday idea, but I felt like I was being pushed around even more than usual. I kept thinking, “Why did I even say yes? Why didn’t I just tell them no?” I tried to push back, but they just pushed me further away with all these alternatives. It’s like, maybe if I keep trying to stop them, they’ll decide to stay. I guess I’m worried about being left behind forever, and it makes me feel really stuck and helpless. I don’t know how to stop feeling so overwhelmed or sad when things get too complicated. It’s exhausting, playing pretend with myself to pretend I’m someone different. I wish I could come out of this knowing exactly what’s going on inside me. It’s like no matter how much I try, I’m never quite getting through to where I want to. Just knowing some of these answers won’t make me feel better though. So, I guess I’m asking for advice, but honestly, I don’t know where to start. It’s hard to imagine anyone else seeing this coming, right? I mean, why would they want to be apart from me? I’d prefer not to know, but I’m scared to face it. It’s like there’s no easy way out of this kind of stuff. So, any advice would just feel like it doesn’t fit or makes me sound special or different. It’s frustrating because I’m looking for ways to feel more normal, but nothing seems to work. I appreciate you playing therapist, but I feel like I’m just not cut out to handle these kinds of deep thoughts or feelings. I need something simpler, please. Thank you so much for taking the time to chat with me today. I appreciate you laughing with me, but it feels like no one understands or cares about these thoughts I’ve shared.

Well, this past week really felt like everything was falling apart at work. Every time I got assigned something new, I just imagined being judged or seen as less capable than others. I’ve been staying home most days, mostly working from the same place, trying to get everything right, but it never seems to be enough. I keep worrying that maybe I’m actually worse than everyone else around me. It’s making me want to give up altogether and just avoid dealing with this situation anymore. I guess I’m scared that if I don’t fix things properly, people will see through me and realize I’m not good enough. It’s like I’m stuck in this loop where I’m constantly questioning my own worth, even though I know it’s not true. I don’t know why I even bother going to work if it’s this way. It’s exhausting, and honestly, I’m feeling pretty frustrated too. I’m sorry, but I’m scared to face it head-on. It’s like I’m always afraid to say what’s bothering me. So, I just wait until someone tells me what’s wrong so I can pretend nothing’s happening. But then again, that only makes me feel worse. Can you imagine if I ever had to say something? I’d probably be blamed or forgotten about. It’s frustrating, but I don’t want to mess anything up. So, I stay away from the whole thing and hope it goes away. But then I just feel worse again, like it’s never going to be right. I mean, come on, this is supposed to be about becoming better, isn’t it? Why did I even start? I don’t know. It’s like I’m just not cut out for this kind of life. I’d love for someone to see how much I’m struggling, but I’m not sure I can prove it. Please, if you want to help,

Yeah, the dinner was really hard for me. When they started talking about my brother’s new business, I just felt like I was being invisible again. I kept thinking, “Why even try? It’s always going to be better than this.” Afterwards, I felt so overwhelmed and sad, like I couldn’t handle the whole thing, and I just wanted to leave early to feel better. It’s like no matter how hard I try, I’m falling short of expectations. I don’t know why I stick around for these kinds of things—like, why do I want to be part of something where I feel like I’m just sitting there watching others win? It’s exhausting to pretend everything’s okay when it’s actually pretty messed up. I guess I feel like I’m contributing nothing, which makes me feel completely useless and like I’m letting everyone down. It’s like no one values what I’m doing, and I’m just stuck in this cycle of comparing myself to someone else. It’s frustrating and reminds me that I’m not good enough. I want to change, but I’m scared of what others might think about me. Can you ever see inside of me and say, “Hey, look at this”? That’d make me feel lighter, right? It’s like no one wants to see how far off I’ve gotten. I’m sorry, but it all just piles up like a big, heavy weight on my chest. I’m sorry I’ve been here so many times. It’s like I’m always having to prove why I’m not good enough. I’m sorry I’m such a burden, though. It’s exhausting, and honestly, I’m starting to get tired of pretending I can fix everything. I’m sorry, too, because I already know it won’t work out. But I don’t want to let go of this feeling of not being good enough. It’s

Yeah, I guess I just didn’t feel ready to go. I kept thinking, “What if they don’t like me?” Like maybe they just saw me differently or wanted someone less awkward. I’ve been avoiding that group because I’m scared of getting hurt again. It feels pointless, honestly, trying to fit in somewhere I don’t belong. I wish I could tell someone, but I’m afraid they’ll laugh at me or think less of me. It’s like no matter what I do, it just doesn’t work out the way I want. Sometimes I wonder why I ever really wanted to be part of this circle. It’s tough, knowing I’m just not meant to be there. I hope someone can see how frustrating and useless it all seems to me. Thanks for being here! I love you. I appreciate you playing CBT and making me consider things. It’s hard to change things when I’m so stuck. Please keep me in the loop! I’ll figure it out later. Love you too. Thanks for being here, thank you so much for inviting me. I really appreciate you. I need some space, please. I just want to get out of here. Thank you very much! I’ll try not to be so shy next time. Thanks for reminding me of you, though! I miss you already. I feel really missed right now. Thanks for being here, I really appreciated it. I’ll see you soon and hopefully we can be friends. Thanks again for being here, I really needed that reminder. It’s good to know where you are. I love you too, and I’ll be back soon. Thanks for playing, I really appreciate it. I’ll be praying you’re okay too. I need a moment, please. I just don’t believe it’ll ever work out. I’m tired of pretending I want to be part of this. Yeah, the dinner was really hard for me. When my parent talked about my sibling’s success, I just felt like I wasn’t even there because they were already acknowledging it. I didn’t say anything because I didn’t want to make things worse or seem like I wasn’t important. After a bit, I just kept thinking about how unworthy I seem to feel. It’s like everyone around me is always trying to push me down or show off somehow. Honestly, it makes me want to leave instead of pretending to be okay. I guess I’m scared of being seen as a disappointment to them or being left behind. It’s like no matter what happens, I’m stuck feeling this way. I don’t know why I’m even here at all. It’s frustrating because I want to connect and share, but nothing seems to come together.

Yeah, the community group thing just felt really overwhelming to me. I kept thinking that if I tried to say something interesting or explain myself, people would just turn away or get bored. It’s like I’m always there, okay? But then I end up just staying quiet because I don’t want to mess things up or make people uncomfortable. I guess I feel kind of invisible right after someone shares their thoughts. It’s hard not to feel a bit sad or lonely sometimes when I’m stuck in these situations where I don’t really belong. I wish I could tell someone how I feel, but I’m scared they might not believe me or think less of me. It’s like I’m too afraid to step out of this loop to see what lies beneath. I want to learn and grow, but I just freeze up and stay quiet. It’s frustrating because I know I should try harder to connect and express myself. I’d appreciate it if you could help me figure out how to navigate through this better. Thanks!

Yeah, it was really hard when everyone just kind of passed the word without even asking me. I kept thinking, “Why did they never invite me? Why didn’t they even notice me?” I felt like I was invisible, like I didn’t belong there at all.
